text: Ni beni ame̱bote ame̱mũsũṅ le̱, anaa ake̱ ame̱yeame̱; ši moṅ ame̱be ye̱ nyãṅemo̱ lolo tamo̱ tšutšu le̱ noṅṅ. Ke̱ke̱ ni mihĩe tšẽ.
translated: And when they entered their room, they found themselves there; but they were no longer in the same place as before. Then I woke up.

ref: I also dreamed that I saw seven heads of grain growing on one stalk. The heads were full and ripe.
text: Ni misā mikwe̱ ye̱ milamõ le̱ mli, ni na, abletšei kpawo ba tšo kome yi, eba kpokplō.
translated: And I looked again in the dream, and behold, seven ears of corn came up on one stalk, and they were plump.

ref: These heads of grain swallowed the full ones. I told my dreams to the magicians, but none of them could tell me the meaning of the dreams.
text: Ni abletšei legelegei le̱ mĩ abletšei kpakpai kpawo le̱. Ni midšadše ene̱ mitšõ woloṅlelo̱i le̱, ši mokomoko enaa he eko ekē̱ē̱mi.
translated: And bad apples are among seven good apples. And I tried to teach the students, but no one understood it at all.

ref: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
text: Ni tšinai kpawo ní etrã lõ, ni he yo̱ nyãṅemo̱, ni nyie ame̱se̱ ba le̱, afii kpawoṅ, ni abletšei kpawo ni noko be̱ yi ni bokã kō̱yo egbĩame̱ keklē le̱, hõmo̱yeli afii kpawo ni.
translated: And seven fat cows that were grazing, and were beautiful, and came up from the river, seven years, and seven thin cows that had nothing on their bodies and were extremely ugly, seven years of

ref: You are now governor of all Egypt!”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Na, miṅõo mito Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱. Bo̱f.
translated: And Pharaoh said to Joseph: Now, give my people land in Egypt. Enough.

ref: He gave Joseph the Egyptian name Zaphenath Paneah. And he let him marry Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, a priest in the city of Heliopolis. Joseph traveled all over Egypt.
text: Ni Farao wo Iosef gbe̱i ake̱ Zafenat-Panea; ni eṅõ On oso̱fo Potifera biyō Asenat le̱ ehãle̱ gblã. Ni Iosef dše kpo eyakpa Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.
translated: And Pharaoh named Joseph Zaphnath-Paaneah; and he gave him Asenath, daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, as wife. And Joseph went out to pass through the land of Egypt.

ref: In fact, there was so much grain that they stopped keeping record, because it was like counting the grains of sand along the beach.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef bua able nā eto babaō dientše̱ take̱ ṅšo̱ nā šia, ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni efõ kanemo̱; edšake̱ ebe̱ yikule̱.
translated: And Joseph was able to make many things himself, like a carpenter, until the time came for him to leave; because he was going to school.

ref: But Jacob did not send Joseph's younger brother Benjamin with them; he was afraid that something might happen to him.
text: Ši Iosef nyemi Benyamin le̱, Iakob hãã eke̱ enyemimei le̱ aya; edšake̱ ekē̱: Koni aleenõ ošrã ko akayašrãle̱.
translated: But Joseph's brother Benjamin, Jacob sent his brothers; because he said: Lest you be robbed on the way.

ref: “No sir,” they replied. “We're your servants, and we have only come to buy grain.
text: Ni ame̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Dabi, minuṅtšo̱, ši niyenii hemo̱ otšũdši ba.
translated: And they answered: No, I am not lying, but food for the work is coming.

ref: We're honest men, and we come from the same family—we're not spies.”
text: Wo̱fẽ nū kome bii dšiwo̱; ano̱kwafoi dšiwo̱, dšee sralo̱i dši otšũdši.
translated: We are one people; we are loyal, not strangers are the inhabitants.

ref: But they explained, “Sir, we come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and one of our brothers is dead.”
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Otšũdši le̱ nyemimei nyoṅma ke̱ enyo̱ ni, nū kome bii dšiwo̱ ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱; ni na, gbekẽ le̱ ye̱ wo̱tše̱ ṅõ amrõ ne̱, ni mokome be̱.
translated: And they said: The twelve brothers and servants were the children of one man in the land of Canaan; and behold, the child is with our father now, and no one is there.

ref: Joseph replied: It's like I said. You're spies,
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Ene̱ mina mi mikē̱nye akē̱: Sralo̱i dšinye lē̱!
translated: And Joseph said to him: You know me, I know that my guests are here!

ref: They did not know that Joseph could understand them, since he was speaking through an interpreter.
text: Ši ame̱ le̱ ame̱lee ake̱ Iosef enu noni ame̱ke̱ le̱; edšake̱ nādšielo̱ dšieo̱ ame̱nā.
translated: But they did not know that Joseph understood what they were saying; because an interpreter was between them.

ref: Joseph turned away from them and cried, but soon he turned back and spoke to them again. Then he had Simeon tied up and taken away while they watched. Joseph's Brothers Return to Canaan
text: Ni etšṍ ehe ke̱-dše ame̱ṅõ ni eyafó; ni ekũ ese̱ eba ame̱ṅõ ekoṅ ni ekeame̱ bawie; ni edšie Simeon ye̱ ame̱teṅ, ni efĩle ye̱ ame̱hĩe.
translated: And he withdrew from among them and departed; and he returned again among them and spoke to them; and he took Simeon from among them, and bound him in their sight.

ref: But we told him, “We're honest men, not spies.
text: Ni wo̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ano̱kwafoi dšiwo̱, wo̱dšee sralo̱i;
translated: And we replied that: We are the true people, we are not strangers;

ref: When the brothers started emptying their sacks of grain, they found their moneybags in them. They were frightened, and so was their father Jacob,
text: Ni eha mli ake̱, beni ame̱dšie ame̱kotokui le̱ amli nii le̱, na, mofẽmo šika ni afĩ le̱ ye̱ ekotoku le̱ mli. Ni beni ame̱na ame̱sika ni afĩmo̱fĩmo̱i le̱, ame̱ke̱ ame̱tše̱ fẽ še gbeyei.
translated: And it happened that, when they took out the contents of their bags, behold, each person had tied money in their bag. And when they saw their tied money, they all became very afraid.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: And their father Jacob said to them: Please, do not take from me: Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and you would take Benjamin also; all these things are against me!

ref: Judah replied, “The governor strictly warned us that we would not be allowed to see him unless we brought our youngest brother with us. If you let us take Benjamin along, we will go and buy grain. But we won't go without him!”
text: ši ke̱dši ohãṅ eya le̱, no le̱ wo̱woṅ yā; edšake̱ nū le̱ kē̱wo̱ ake̱: Nyenaṅ mihĩe, akē̱ dša nyenyemi le̱ fata nyehe.
translated: But when the sun set, then the moon appeared; because the water said: You are beautiful, and your sister is also beautiful.

ref: Their father said: If Benjamin must go with you, take the governor a gift of some of the best things from our own country, such as perfume, honey, spices, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Israel kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Ke̱dši nakai le̱, no le̱ bo̱ni nyefe ne̱: nyeṅõa šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱ yibii kpakpai le̱ eko nyewoa nyenibii amli, ni *nyeke̱-yakea nū le̱, gbonyowō tšofã fiō ke̱ wò fiō ke̱ tšofã-ke̱-ṅma ke̱ ohe̱ ni dšeo̱ ṅma, ke̱ amitšro̱bii ke̱ ṅme̱i; *Abe̱i
translated: And the people of Israel said to them: If that is the case, then this is what you must do: you must take some of the good fruits of the land and put them in your baskets,

ref: he saw Benjamin and told the servant in charge of his house, “Take these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and cook it, so they can eat with me at noon.”
text: Ni beni Iosef na ake̱ Benyamin fata ame̱he le̱, ekē̱ ešĩa le̱ no̱ onukpa le̱ ake̱: Oke̱ ne̱kē̱ hĩĩ ne aya šĩa ni ogbe koloi oke̱-fe niyenii oto; ši hĩĩ ne̱ ke̱mi baye nii ſane ne̱.
translated: When Joseph saw that Benjamin was with them, he asked their elder, saying: Why should you go and steal and kill animals to prepare food for yourselves? But it is better for me to eat my own

ref: together with enough money to buy more grain. We don't know who put the money in our sacks.”
text: ni šika kroko hũ wo̱ke̱-hĩe wo̱dẽ ke̱-ba niyenii heme̱ ne̱; wo̱lee moni ṅõ wo̱šika le̱ ewo wo̱kotokui le̱ amli.
translated: And with other money too, we went and brought this food; we don't know who took our money and put it in our pockets.

ref: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, “What about your elderly father? Is he still alive?”
text: Ni ebiame̱ bo̱ni ame̱yo̱, ni ekē̱: Ani nyetše̱ nūmo le̱ *ni nyetšĩ etā le̱ ye̱ dšogbāṅ lo? Ani eye̱ dšeṅ lolo? *
translated: And he asked them, saying: Have you had one and done three secretly? Is it bad then?

ref: After washing his face and returning, he was able to control himself and said, “Serve the meal!”
text: Ni efo̱ ehĩe ni edše kpo, ni emĩa ehĩe, ni ekē̱: Nyeke̱ niyenii abamãmo̱a ši!
translated: And he washed his face and came out, and he wiped his face, and he said: Let no food be served!

ref: But they had not gone far from the city when Joseph told the servant, “Go after those men! When you catch them, say, ‘My master has been good to you. So why have you stolen his silver cup?
text: Beni ame̱dše kpo ye̱ mã le̱ṅ ni ame̱še̱ko šoṅṅ heko le̱, ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef kē̱ ešĩa le̱ no̱ onukpa le̱ ake̱: Te ši, ni otiu gbomei le̱, ni ké̱ oninaame̱ le̱, kē̱mo̱ame̱ ake̱: Menihewo̱ nyeke̱ efõṅ to ekpakpa nãdšiaṅ ne̱?
translated: When they had gone out of the city and had not yet gone far, then Joseph said to his steward: Pursue them, and when you overtake them, and when you find them, say to them: Why have you repaid evil for good?

ref: Not only does he drink from his cup, but he also uses it to learn about the future. You have done a terrible thing.’ ”
text: Té ene̱ mli minuṅtšo̱ nuo̱ dā̃ ye̱, ni esā ekē̱-klaä? Nyefe efõṅ ake̱ nyefe nakai le̱!
translated: How can this minister be drinking water and then deny it? You are wrong to do that!

ref: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
text: Ni ebṍi taomo̱ ke̱-dše̱ onukpa le̱ no̱ ni eyagbe nā ye̱ gbekẽ le̱ no̱; ni ayana kpulu le̱ ye̱ Benyamin kotoku le̱ mli.
translated: And he asked for the elder's portion and gave to the younger one's portion; and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.

ref: Judah went over to Joseph and said: Sir, you have as much power as the king himself, and I am only your slave. Please don't get angry if I speak.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iuda tši be̱ṅke̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Ofai ne̱, minuṅtšo̱! Hã ní otšũlo̱ awie wiemo̱ ko ye̱ minuṅtšo̱ toiiaṅ; ni kãhã omli mīwo otšũlo̱ la, ši otamo̱ Farao noṅṅ!
translated: Then Judah approached and said: Oh my lord, my master! Please let your servant speak a word in my master's ears, and do not let your anger burn against your servant, for you are like Pharaoh himself!

ref: Then he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him and told about it in the king's palace.
text: Ni ewó egbē ni efó, ni Mizraimbii ke̱ Farao webii le̱ tete nu.
translated: And it was a heavy burden that the Egyptians and Pharaoh's people bore.

ref: Joseph was still crying as he kissed each of his other brothers. After this, they started talking with Joseph.
text: Ni esā eſo̱ſō̱ enyemimei le̱ fẽ nā, ni efó eſie ame̱no̱; ni no se̱ le̱ enyemimei le̱ ke̱le̱ wie.
translated: And he gathered all his disciples, and he went among them; and after that, his disciples spoke.

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Ni anu sane ne̱ ye̱ Farao we le̱ ake̱: Iosef nyemimei eba! Ni Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱ ná be mīše̱.
translated: And the news reached Pharaoh's house that: Joseph's brothers have come! And Pharaoh said to his servants, "Hurry up.

ref: So the king said to Joseph: Tell your brothers to load their donkeys and return to Canaan.
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Kē̱mo̱ onyemimei le̱ ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyetẽrea nyekoloi le̱ adšatšui, ni nyeyiaṅ ke̱-yaä Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱,
translated: And Pharaoh said to Joseph: Tell your brothers that: This is what you must do: load your animals with provisions, and go to the land of Canaan.

ref: Also tell your brothers to take some wagons from Egypt for their wives and children to ride in. And be sure they bring their father.
text: Ni bo le̱ afā̃o ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyeṅõa ſīlii ke̱-dše̱a Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱ nyebāa nyebii ke̱ nyeṅãmei, ni nyeyaṅõa nyetše̱ ke̱-baä.
translated: And you will say to them that: What you will do is this: you will take silver from the land of Egypt and you will buy your children and your wives, and you will redeem yourselves.

ref: Jacob's sons agreed to do what the king had said. And Joseph gave them wagons and food for their trip home, just as the king had ordered.
text: Ni Israel bihĩĩ le̱ fe nakai. Ni Iosef hāame̱ ſīlii; take̱ boni Farao fále̱ le̱, ni ehãame̱ gbe̱no̱ niyenii.
translated: And the children of Israel treated him so. And Joseph gave him silver; but when Pharaoh bought him, he gave him food later.

ref: Sixty-six members of Jacob's family went to Egypt with him, not counting his daughters-in-law.
text: Susumai fē ni ke̱ Iakob ba Mizraim ni dše̱ ehe̱ mli ne̱, sõro ebii le̱ aṅãmei le̱, *susumai le̱ fẽ dši nyoṅmai-ekpa ke̱ ekpa.
translated: All the souls who came with Jacob to Egypt and were born of him, besides his children and their wives, all the souls were sixty-six.

ref: I will let them live anywhere they choose in the land of Egypt, but I suggest that they settle in Goshen, the best part of our land. I would also like for your finest shepherds to watch after my own sheep and goats.”
text: Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ kã ohĩe; šikpoṅ le̱, beni hĩ fè fẽ ni dši Gošen šikpoṅ le̱, hã otše̱ ke̱ onyemimei le̱ ahĩ dše̱i; ni ke̱dši ole ame̱teṅ meikomei ṅí dši gbomei ni sa le̱, no le̱ ṅõame̱ ofe oṅukpai ye̱ mikoloi le̱ ano̱.
translated: The land of Egypt is before you; the land, where it is best, which is the land of Goshen, let your father and your brothers dwell there; and if you know among them

ref: “If you don't have any money,” Joseph answered, “give me your animals, and I'll let you have some grain.”
text: Ni Iosef ké̱: Ni nyešika etã ne̱, nyeṅõa nyekoloi nyehãa, ni mahãnye ye̱ nyekoloi le̱ anā.
translated: And Joseph said: I have three coins, I will give you my clothes, and I will give you my clothes for free.

ref: “Will you give me your word?” Jacob asked. “Yes, I will,” Joseph promised. After this, Jacob bowed down and prayed at the head of his bed.
text: Ni eké̱: Kãmi kitã! Ni ekále̱ kitã. Ni Israel mã eyi ši ye̱ sātšo le̱ yiteṅ.
translated: And he said: Let us fight! And he fought. And Israel's mother was in the midst of the battle.

ref: Then Joseph made his sons move away from Jacob's knees, and Joseph bowed down in front of him with his face to the ground.
text: Ni Iosef dšieame̱ ke̱-dše̱ enakutšei ateṅ ke̱-ba, ni eho ekoto ši, eṅõ ehĩe ebu ši.
translated: And Joseph was brought out of the dungeon, and he shaved his head, and changed his clothes.

ref: You will tie your donkey to a choice grapevine and wash your clothes in wine from those grapes.
text: Eefĩ etedšibi eṅmõ weintšo ke̱ etedšiyō bi hũ eṅmõ weintšonine; eke̱ wein aafo̱ etade he, ni ehebo le̱ eke̱ weinyibii amli lá aafo̱ he. Ioel
translated: The worm eats the fruit of the vine, and the caterpillar also eats its leaves; and the locust will destroy its bark, and the cankerworm will destroy its shoots and its inner core.

ref: Naphtali, you are a wild deer with lovely fawns.
text: Naftali le̱, ofrote ni he yo̱ oyá ni, ni ewieo̱ wiemo̱i ke̱ ṅõmo̱i. Kods.
translated: Naphtali is a swift deer, quick, and it utters graceful words. Genesis.

ref: Joseph, you are a fruitful vine growing near a stream and climbing a wall.
text: Iosef le̱, tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, nu ni baä he tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, ni enidši le̱ kwō̱lo̱ö̱ gbogbo. Hos.

text: Abō̱ le̱ ke̱ te̱kplo ni yo̱ mli le̱ ahe ye̱ Hetbii le̱ adeṅ.
translated: The baobab tree and the contents of its fruit are used in the preparation of medicine.

ref: When the time of mourning was over, Joseph said to the Egyptian leaders, “If you consider me your friend, please speak to the king for me.
text: Ni beni efómo̱ gbĩĩ le̱ ebo le̱, Iosef ke̱ Farao we le̱ bii le̱ wie ni ekē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ke̱dši mihĩe eba nyam ye̱ nyehĩe le̱, mīkpanye fai, nyewoa Farao toi no̱ ake.
translated: And when the famine was severe in the land, Joseph spoke to Pharaoh's household and said: Now if I have found favor in your eyes, I beg you, speak to Pharaoh on my behalf.

ref: You tried to harm me, but God made it turn out for the best, so that he could save all these people, as he is now doing.
text: Nye le̱, nyesusumi efõṅ; ši *Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, esusule̱ ekpakpa, beni afe ni ahere mei babaõ yiwala take̱ bo̱ni eba mli ùmene ne.
translated: You, your thoughts are evil; but God, His thoughts are good, so that many people might be saved, just as it has come to pass today.

ref: If we don't outsmart them, their families will keep growing larger. And if our country goes to war, they could easily fight on the side of our enemies and escape from Egypt.
text: Nyebaä, nyehãa woke̱ame̱ ayea ṅā mli, koni ame̱yi akafá ní ebaba mli ake, ké̱ ta ko bate ši le̱, ni ame̱ hũ ame̱yafata wo̱henye̱lo̱i le̱ ahe ní ame̱ke̱wo̱ ṅwu ní ame̱mīfã ke̱-ṅdše šikpoṅ le̱ no̱!
translated: You come, let us go and eat there, so that they don't get tired, and it will come to pass that, if a war breaks out, they too will join our enemies and fight

ref: But even though the Israelites were mistreated, their families grew larger, and they took over more land. Because of this, the Egyptians feared them worse than before
text: Ši babaō ní ame̱waäame̱ yi le̱, babaō ní ame̱yi fáa ní ame̱ſereo̱ dši no, ni ame̱tšui suo̱ Israelbii le̱ ano̱.
translated: But many of them perished, and many of them were not pleased, and their hearts were against the Israelites.

ref: and made them work so hard
text: Ni Mizraimbii le̱ hĩe Israelbii le̱ nyṍdšiahĩemo̱ še̱ī,
translated: And the Egyptians had darkness and trouble before the Israelites.

ref: The king called them in again and asked, “Why are you letting those baby boys live?”
text: Ni Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ tšé̱ yeife̱yei le̱ ni ekē̱ame̱ ake: Mẽhewo̱ nyefe nakai ni nyehere gbekẽbiihĩĩ le̱ ayiwala le̱?
translated: And the king of Egypt called the women and said to them: Why did you do that and save the male children alive?

ref: Some shepherds tried to chase them away, but Moses came to their rescue and watered their animals.
text: Ši tōkwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba baſieame̱; ni Mose te ši, ni efã amehe, ni *ehã ametōi le̱ anu. *
translated: And the quails came and covered them; and Moses stood up, and he interceded for them, and granted their desires.

ref: After the death of the king of Egypt, the Israelites still complained because they were forced to be slaves. They cried out for help,
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ nakai gbĩĩ pī le̱ amli le̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ bagbo, ni Israelbii le̱ dõmo̱ ntšo̱i ye̱ nyṍṅyeli le̱ hewo, ni ame̱fó ame̱tšé̱ Nyoṅmo̱; ni ame-nyṍṅyeli le̱ mli fómo̱-ke̱-tšémo̱ le̱ *yase̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ṅõ. *
translated: And it came to pass in those many days that the king of Egypt died, and the children of Israel groaned because of the bondage, and they cried out to God; and their cry from the bondage reached God.

ref: and God heard their loud cries. He did not forget the promise he had made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob,
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ nu ame̱ṅke̱mo̱yeli blo̱mo̱ le̱, ni Nyoṅmo̱ kai *ekpãṅmo̱ ní eke̱ Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob kpãṅ le̱; *
translated: And God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant which he made with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob.

ref: Call together the leaders of Israel and tell them that the God who was worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Tell them I have seen how terribly they are being treated in Egypt,
text: Ya ní oyabua Israel onukpai le̱ anā, ni okē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa, nyetše̱mei a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, ní dši Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, edšie ehe kpo etšõmi, ni ekē̱: Sramo̱ mibasranye, ni mina noni afeo̱nye ye̱ Mizraim le̱;
translated: Go and gather the elders of Israel and tell them that: Jehovah, the God of your fathers, who is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, has appeared to me, and said: I have surely visited you, and seen what is done to you in Egypt;

ref: The Lord sent Aaron to meet Moses in the desert. So Aaron met Moses at Mount Sinai and greeted him with a kiss.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Aaron ake̱: Ya ní oke̱ Mose ayakpe ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱. Ni ete ní eke̱le̱ yakpe ye̱ Nyoṅmo̱ goṅ le̱ he, ni eſo̱ enā.
translated: And Jehovah said to Aaron: Go and tell Moses to meet him there. And he will be able to meet before God, and he will see.

ref: The king said, “Moses and Aaron, why are you keeping these people from working? Look how many you are keeping from doing their work. Now everyone get back to work!”
text: Ni Farao sā kē̱: Na, agbe̱ne̱ maṅ le̱ *yi efa momo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni nyehã ame̱dšo̱ö̱ amehe noṅṅ ne̱! *
translated: And Pharaoh said: Behold, this people is already numerous in this land, and let us deal shrewdly with them!

ref: That same day the king gave orders to his Egyptian slave bosses and to the Israelite men directly in charge of the Israelite slaves. He told them:
text: Ni nakai gbĩ le̱ no̱ noṅṅ le̱ Farao wo maṅ le̱ kplalo̱i ke̱ ame̱no̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ akitã ake̱:
translated: And on that day, Pharaoh gave another command to the overseers and foremen of the land, saying:

ref: Don't give the slaves any more straw to put in their bricks. Force them to find their own straw wherever they can,
text: Nyekahãa maṅ le̱ dſe̱i ame̱ṅõ-fe ntayāi le̱ doṅṅ tamo̱ tšutšu le̱; ame̱dientše̱ ameya ní ame̱yabuabua dſe̱i anā ame̱hã amehe;
translated: Don't let the nation fall into a trap of their own making like a bird; they themselves went and built a trap for themselves;

ref: Make them work so hard that they won't have time to listen to these lies.
text: Ahã nitšumo̱ le̱ anyẽ gbomei le̱, koni ato̱ame̱ ní ame̱kabo yakawiemo̱i atoi!
translated: This work has made some people firm so that they don't listen to evil talk!

ref: The bosses beat the men in charge of the slaves and said, “Why didn't you force the slaves to make as many bricks yesterday and today as they did before?”
text: Ni ayi Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i ní Farao kplalo̱i le̱ ṅõ-to ame̱no̱ le̱ ake̱: Mẽhewo̱ ni nyè ke̱ ṅme̱ne̱ fẽ nyegbee nyentayāi ní atonye le̱ anā tamo̱ sá le̱?
translated: And the Israelite foremen whom Pharaoh's taskmasters had appointed over them, were beaten and asked them, saying: Why have you not completed your quota of bricks today as before?

ref: Finally, the men in charge of the slaves went to the king and said, “Why are you treating us like this?
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba ni ame̱bablo̱ ame̱wo Farao ame̱kē̱: Mẽhewo̱ oke̱ otšũdši yeo̱ ne̱ke̱?
translated: Then the overseers of the Israelites came and complained to Pharaoh, saying: Why are your servants treated like this?

ref: Then the men said, “We hope the Lord will punish both of you for making the king and his officials hate us. Now they even have an excuse to kill us.” The Lord's Promise to Moses
text: ni ame̱kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa aakwe̱ ní ekodšo, edšake̱ nyehã wohe *edše fũ ewo Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱, ni nyeṅõ klante nyewo ame̱dẽ ní ame̱ke̱-gbewo̱. *
translated: And they said to them: Jehovah will fight and deliver, because you have made us a stench to Pharaoh and his servants, and you have put a sword in their hand to kill us.

ref: But the Lord sent Aaron and Moses with a message for the Israelites and for the king. He also ordered Aaron and Moses to free the people from Egypt. Family Record of Aaron and Moses
text: Ni Iehowa ke̱ Mose ke̱ Aaron wie, ni efã́ame̱ ake̱ ameya Israelbii le̱ ke̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ Farao le̱ ṅõ, ní ame̱ya-dšie Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱.
translated: And Jehovah spoke to Moses and Aaron, and commanded them to go to the Israelites and to Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, and to bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt.

ref: Merari's sons were Mahli and Mushi. All of the above were from the Levi tribe.
text: Ni Merari bii ne̱: Maheli ke̱ Muši. Mene̱mei dši Levi wekui le̱ ye̱ ame̱fo̱mo̱i le̱ anā.
translated: And Merari's children were: Mahli and Mushi. These were the families of Levi according to their generations.

ref: Korah's sons were Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph.
text: Ni Kora bii ne̱: Asir ke̱ Elkana ke̱ Abiasaf. Mene̱mei dši Korabii wekui le̱.
translated: Korah's sons were: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These were the Korahite families.

ref: The Lord had commanded Aaron and Moses to lead every family and tribe of Israel out of Egypt,
text: Mene̱mei dši Aaron ke̱ Mose ni Iehowa kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyeyadšiea Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ye̱ ame̱tai le̱ anā le̱.
translated: These were Aaron and Moses, and Jehovah said to them: Deliver the Israelites from the land of Egypt from their oppression.

ref: he said, “I am the Lord. Tell the king of Egypt everything I say to you.”
text: ake̱ Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Midši Iehowa le̱; kē̱mo̱ Farao, Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱, nii fẽ ní mike̱o wieo̱ le̱.
translated: Jehovah said to Moses: I am Jehovah; and Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, will know that I am Jehovah.

ref: The king behaved just as the Lord had said and stubbornly refused to listen. The Nile River Turns into Blood
text: Ni awadše Farao tšui, ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ bo̱ni Iehowa *ewie le̱. *
translated: And Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he refused to listen, just as Jehovah had spoken.

ref: Tomorrow morning take the stick that turned into a snake, then wait beside the Nile River for the king.
text: Ya Farao ṅõ lebi; na, ēdše kpo eya fā le̱ nā, ni oyadamo̱ fā le̱ nā omẽle̱, ni tšo le̱ ní tšṍ onũfũ le̱ ko̱ ohĩe odẽ,
translated: And Pharaoh's heart was hardened; and he refused to let them go, and you yourself refused to let them go, and you showed them no mercy at all.

ref: “The Lord is going to do something to show you that he really is the Lord. I will strike the Nile with this stick, and the water will turn into blood.
text: Boni Iehowa kē̱ ne̱: Ene̱ oke̱-ale ake̱ midši Iehowa le̱: na, mike̱ tšo ní mihĩe ye̱ midẽ ne̱ batſa fā le̱ mli nu le̱, ni *eetšṍ lá. *
translated: As Jehovah said: By this you will know that I am Jehovah: and I will take the staff that is in my hand and strike the water of the Nile, and it will turn into blood.

ref: the fish died, and the water smelled so bad that none of the Egyptians could drink it. Blood was everywhere in Egypt.
text: Ni lōi ní yo̱ fā le̱ mli le̱ gboi, ni fā le̱ ša, ni Mizraimbii le̱ nyẽẽ fā le̱ mli nu le̱ anu; ni lá so̱ṅṅ yo̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ.
translated: And the fish that were in the river died, and the river stank, and the Egyptians could not drink the water from the river; and blood was everywhere in the land of Egypt.
